_comment: DO NOT EDIT: FILE GENERATED AUTOMATICALLY BY PTERODACTYL PANEL - PTERODACTYL.IO
name: Neoforge
author: support@phosphor.host
description: Minecraft Neoforge Server. Minecraft Neoforge is a modding API (Application Programming Interface), which makes it easier to create mods, and also make sure mods are compatible with each other.

installation.script:
#!/bin/bash
# NeoForge Installation Script
#
# Server Files: /mnt/server
apt update
apt install -y curl jq

if [[ ! -d /mnt/server ]]; then
  mkdir /mnt/server
fi

cd /mnt/server

# Remove spaces from the version numbers to avoid issues with curl
NEOFORGE_VERSION="$(echo "$NEOFORGE_VERSION" | tr -d ' ')"
MC_VERSION="$(echo "$MC_VERSION" | tr -d ' ')"

API_SITE="https://maven.neoforged.net/api/maven/versions/releases/net/neoforged/neoforge"
FILE_SITE="https://maven.neoforged.net/releases/net/neoforged/neoforge"

if [[ ! -z "${NEOFORGE_VERSION}" ]]; then
  echo -e "Using explicit NeoForge version ${NEOFORGE_VERSION}"
else
  ## Grab the full list of published NeoForge versions
  VERSIONS=$(curl -sSL "${API_SITE}" | jq -r '.versions[]')

  ## Build a version prefix from the requested Minecraft version.
  ## NeoForge versions are <minor>.<patch>.<build> and map to Minecraft 1.<minor>.<patch>
  ## e.g. MC 1.21.10 -> 21.10.* , MC 1.21 -> 21.0.*
  if [[ "${MC_VERSION}" == "latest" ]] || [[ "${MC_VERSION}" == "" ]]; then
    echo -e "getting latest version of neoforge."
    FILTER=""
  else
    MC_TRIM="${MC_VERSION#1.}"
    if [[ "${MC_TRIM}" == *.* ]]; then
      FILTER="${MC_TRIM}."
    else
      FILTER="${MC_TRIM}.0."
    fi
  fi

  if [[ "${BUILD_TYPE}" != "recommended" ]] && [[ "${BUILD_TYPE}" != "latest" ]]; then
    BUILD_TYPE=recommended
  fi

  echo -e "minecraft version: ${MC_VERSION}"
  echo -e "build type: ${BUILD_TYPE}"

  ## recommended = newest stable build, latest = newest build including betas
  if [[ "${BUILD_TYPE}" == "latest" ]]; then
    NEOFORGE_VERSION=$(echo "${VERSIONS}" | grep -E "^${FILTER}" | tail -1)
  else
    NEOFORGE_VERSION=$(echo "${VERSIONS}" | grep -E "^${FILTER}" | grep -v -- '-beta' | tail -1)
    ## drop back to including betas if there is no stable build yet
    if [[ "${NEOFORGE_VERSION}" == "" ]]; then
      echo -e "dropping back to latest from recommended due to there not being a stable version of neoforge for the mc version requested."
      NEOFORGE_VERSION=$(echo "${VERSIONS}" | grep -E "^${FILTER}" | tail -1)
    fi
  fi
fi

## Error if no valid version was found.
if [ "${NEOFORGE_VERSION}" == "" ] || [ "${NEOFORGE_VERSION}" == "null" ]; then
  echo -e "The install failed because there is no valid version of neoforge for the version of minecraft selected."
  exit 1
fi

#Adding .jar when not eding by SERVER_JARFILE
if [[ ! $SERVER_JARFILE = *\.jar ]]; then
  SERVER_JARFILE="$SERVER_JARFILE.jar"
fi

DOWNLOAD_LINK=${FILE_SITE}/${NEOFORGE_VERSION}/neoforge-${NEOFORGE_VERSION}

#Downloading jars
echo -e "Downloading neoforge version ${NEOFORGE_VERSION}"
echo -e "Download link is ${DOWNLOAD_LINK}-installer.jar"

if curl --output /dev/null --silent --head --fail ${DOWNLOAD_LINK}-installer.jar; then
  echo -e "installer jar download link is valid."
else
  echo -e "link is invalid. Exiting now"
  exit 2
fi

curl -s -o installer.jar -sS ${DOWNLOAD_LINK}-installer.jar

#Checking if downloaded jars exist
if [[ ! -f ./installer.jar ]]; then
  echo "!!! Error downloading neoforge version ${NEOFORGE_VERSION} !!!"
  exit 3
fi

function unix_args {
  echo -e "Setting up neoforge unix args."
  ln -sf libraries/net/neoforged/neoforge/*/unix_args.txt unix_args.txt
}

# Delete args to support downgrading/upgrading
rm -rf libraries/net/neoforged/neoforge
rm -f unix_args.txt

#Installing server
echo -e "Installing neoforge server.\n"
java -jar installer.jar --installServer || { echo -e "\nInstall failed using NeoForge version ${NEOFORGE_VERSION} and Minecraft version ${MC_VERSION}.\nShould you be using unlimited memory value of 0, make sure to increase the default install resource limits in the Wings config or specify exact allocated memory in the server Build Configuration instead of 0! \nOtherwise, the NeoForge installer will not have enough memory."; exit 4; }

# NeoForge always launches through generated JPMS args
if ls libraries/net/neoforged/neoforge/*/unix_args.txt >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  unix_args
else
  echo -e "ERROR: neoforge unix_args.txt was not produced; the install may have failed."
  exit 5
fi

echo -e "Deleting installer.jar file.\n"
rm -rf installer.jar
echo -e "Installation process is completed"

variables.name: Server Jar File
variables.description: The name of the Jarfile to use when running NeoForge. NeoForge 1.20.2+ launches via @unix_args.txt, so this is only a fallback.

variables.name: Minecraft Version
variables.description:
The version of minecraft you want to install for.

Leaving latest will install the latest recommended version.

variables.name: Build Type
variables.description:
The type of NeoForge build to install.

Valid types are "recommended" (newest stable) and "latest" (newest, may include betas).

variables.name: NeoForge Version
variables.description:
The full exact NeoForge version.

Ex. 21.10.64

Overrides MC_VERSION and BUILD_TYPE. If it fails to download the server files it will fail to install.
